scope_summary: 2h fixed-bar regression gate (proving soak) for bl-009 (ipc_mqueue_pset_receive UAF via filt_machport). Fresh-built mach.ko from clean e101f9c, symbol-verified, baked into a disposable image, soaked 2h. PASS: retires op-105, proves bl-009.

validated_fix.fix_subject: mach: pin port-set receive candidate across lock drop
validated_fix.kernel_built_fresh_from: clean e101f9c checkout (wip-rmxos alpha HEAD, working tree clean — no uncommitted changes)

symbol_verification.method: objdump -dr differential on ipc_mqueue.o (NOT hash-compare — FreeBSD .ko builds are non-reproducible; per Arranger correction)
symbol_verification.note: 1 ip_reference (temp-ref before pset lock drop) + 3 ip_release (revalidate-restart path + two exit paths) — exact shape of the e101f9c patch; absent in pre-fix. ip_reference/ip_release compile to io_reference/io_release.

retires.reason: op-105's 2h soak panicked on iteration 1 (Fatal trap 9: general protection fault, ipc_mqueue_pset_receive+0xbf via filt_machport, DEADC0DE poison on a freed mutex). op-108 runs the same 2h config to completion with 0 panic — the acceptance bar op-105 could not meet.

proving_run.base_image: disposable copy of 20260623T055157Z-op108-fixed-op104-smoke/block078-userland-smoke.img (most recent post-op-106 fixed-kernel image)
proving_run.hard_stop_scan_serial: clean (no Fatal trap | panic: | KASSERT | db> | GPF | trap 0x9 | lock order reversal | Signal 12)
proving_run.hard_stop_scan_harness_log: clean (0 panic/GPF/Fatal/trap lines across 300223 harness-log lines)

oracle_end_deltas.port_slope_leak_assessment: FLAT — port delta holds at 2 across all 120 per-minute checkpoints (t=1m delta=2 ... t=120m delta=2). Delta=2 is the expected steady-state (the 2 ports that persist for queue/source lifetime, not a leak). No monotonic growth across 2h -> no port leak. This is the per-iteration steady-state port delta op-105 owed (end-balance alone could not distinguish a leak; ports persist for queue/source lifetime).

parity_with_op105.op105_result: Fatal trap 9 (general protection fault) on iteration 1; ipc_mqueue_pset_receive+0xbf <- filt_machport+0x269 <- kqueue_scan; rcx/rax = deadc0de (freed-memory poison); guest hung at the kernel debugger (db>).
parity_with_op105.op108_result: 27293 iterations, 0 fails, 0 panic, clean 2h completion + poweroff, exit 0.
parity_with_op105.delta: Same 2h soak config (SOAK_DURATION=7200, soak-oracle-2h.d, dispatch-conformance harness driving MACH_RECV create/destroy churn — exactly the load that drives the receive walk + concurrent port frees). Only material difference: op-108 carries the e101f9c fix. The UAF window is closed.

decision.basis: Fresh-built (from clean e101f9c) + symbol-verified mach.ko baked into a disposable image; 2h proving soak completed with 0 panic/GPF (vs op-105 iter-1 panic under the identical config); 27293 iterations 0 fails; oracle END with all 4 deltas; flat port-slope (no leak) across 120 checkpoints; clean poweroff exit 0. bl-009's UAF is gone under the 2h config that surfaced it.
decision.retirement_lane: Gatekeeper proving-soak (fixed-bar regression gate) lane. The e101f9c correctness review (Rule 6, kernel-correctness: ref-leak paths, restart-loop termination, assertion coverage) is a SEPARATE Validators lane that ran in parallel — not gated by op-108 and not adjudicated here.

coverage_caveats.provenance_method: Symbol/objdump differential per Arranger (FreeBSD .ko builds non-reproducible; hash-compare unsound for cross-build provenance). The incidental byte-match of the fresh e101f9c build to 7c8a710d is NOT the adjudicator.
coverage_caveats.fix_soundness_scope: op-108 proves the RACE is gone (the 2h soak that hit the UAF no longer panics). It does NOT adjudicate the fix's broader correctness — that is the Validators' Rule 6 review (ref-leak on all exit paths, restart-loop convergence, assertion coverage), a separate lane.
coverage_caveats.harness_provenance: harness binary + soak-driver.sh + soak-oracle-2h.d are the canonical op-104/op-107 artifacts (base image + explorer repo findings/nx-r64z/dtrace/dispatch-conformance/), reused not improvised — the Arranger's 'wrong staging -> false rmxOS-gap finding' warning respected. The only new element is the freshly-built mach.ko.
